title: Integration of podman with buildroot
body:
User : Manthan Gajera

- This Pull request is regarding to integrate the podman with buildroot.

comments.body:

Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.

You must sign your commmits. Not really sure what buildroot is?

@lsm5 @cevich PTAL
Cockpit tests failed for commit d12ebac280229dfc5c455405de93e9dbd7e034e8. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.

> Thank you for your PR! The big question here is, what is buildroot, and why does Podman need to support it upstream and in top-level files? Secondary question is, who will support this when it breaks or needs new functionality?

Agree with @edsantiago here. @manunixgit Buildroot is probably a very generic term and it's hard to see where and how it will be used from the commit message and diff. And we'd also need to know about maintainer commitment and availability before accepting this.

This packaging information should go in Buildroot, rather than in the upstream repository...

Like: https://github.com/buildroot/buildroot/tree/master/package/docker-engine (and so on)

EDIT: upstream/downstream confusion

The .mk is more like a .spec, if it helps

----

See https://buildroot.org/contribute.html

But you can also add it to your own project.

> This packaging information should go in Buildroot, rather than in the downstream project...
> 
> Like: https://github.com/buildroot/buildroot/tree/master/package/docker (and so on)
> 
> See https://buildroot.org/contribute.html

Thanks for the info @afbjorklund  . Adding it to the buildroot project seems fine by me if someone wants to.

> But you can also add it to your own project.

Rather not, unless 
- adding to the buildroot project does not work well enough 
- we have active maintainers for it participating upstream
- (others may have other concerns)

> > But you can also add it to your own project.

> Rather not,

I did not mean for podman upstream, I meant that the buildroot user can add their own packages using "external"

https://buildroot.org/downloads/manual/manual.html#customize-packages

----


For instance, here is the package used in the minikube.iso (that only uses podman to load images into cri-o storage):

https://github.com/kubernetes/minikube/tree/master/deploy/iso/minikube-iso/package/podman

> > > But you can also add it to your own project.
> 
> > Rather not,
> 
> I did not mean for podman upstream, I meant that the buildroot user can add their own packages using "external"
> 
> https://buildroot.org/downloads/manual/manual.html#customize-packages
> 
> For instance, here is the package used in the minikube.iso (that only uses podman to load images into cri-o storage):
> 
> https://github.com/kubernetes/minikube/tree/master/deploy/iso/minikube-iso/package/podman

Ah, I see what you mean @afbjorklund, thanks! And in that case, I think we can close this issue.

Most likely, any such packaging of podman would also have to include all the dependencies?

* conmon

* crun

* netavark

* catatonit

And the best is to use the language-specific make fragments (Go, Rust, etc) where possible:

* https://github.com/buildroot/buildroot/blob/master/package/containerd/containerd.mk

* https://github.com/buildroot/buildroot/blob/master/package/runc/runc.mk

* https://github.com/buildroot/buildroot/blob/master/package/cni-plugins/cni-plugins.mk

* https://github.com/buildroot/buildroot/blob/master/package/tini/tini.mk
